files.Title: BridgingIT.DevKit.Common.ResultJsonConverter
files.Summary:
A custom JSON converter for that writes a compact and client-safe JSON representation.

Behavior: - Writes the following top-level properties: - isSuccess (boolean) - messages (array of strings) - errors (array of error objects) - For general errors, delegates to using the error's type and the provided options. - For , emits a safe, flattened object and deliberately excludes the raw instance to avoid unsupported types (e.g., MethodBase) and oversized/PII payloads.

Notes: - Deserialization is not supported and will throw . - This converter is intended for outbound API responses or logging, not for round-tripping back into domain models.

files.Title: BridgingIT.DevKit.Common.RuleBuilder
files.Summary:
Provides a fluent interface for building and executing collections of rules.

Usage example: 

return Rule
    // Basic validation using ValueRules
    .Add(Rules.IsNotEmpty(product.Name))
    .Add(Rules.StringLength(product.Name, 3, 100))
    .Add(Rules.NumericRange(product.Price, 0.01m, 999.99m))

    // Conditional validation with When
    .When(!product.IsDigital, builder => builder
        .Add(Rules.IsNotEmpty(product.ShippingAddress))
        .Add(Rules.StringLength(product.ShippingAddress, 10, 200)))

    // Using Unless (inverse of When)
    .Unless(product.IsDigital,
        Rules.GreaterThan(product.Price, 10m))

    // Combining multiple conditions
    .WhenAll(new[]
    {
        product.Price > 100,
        product.IsDigital,
        product.Categories?.Count > 2
    }, Rules.IsNotEmpty(product.ShippingAddress))
    .Check();

files.Title: BridgingIT.DevKit.Common.SagaScope
files.Summary:
Generic saga scope implementation for coordinating distributed transactions using the compensating transaction pattern.

This class orchestrates multi-step workflows where each successful step registers a compensation function. If any step fails, all registered compensations are executed in reverse order (LIFO - Last In First Out) to undo previous changes. This implements the saga pattern for maintaining consistency across distributed operations without traditional two-phase commit.

files.Title: BridgingIT.DevKit.Common.TestHttpMessageHandler
files.Summary:
A test utility class for unit testing HTTP-based services.

TestHttpMessageHandler allows you to inject custom HTTP responses when using in unit tests. This makes it possible to simulate various HTTP scenarios (success, error, etc) without making actual network calls or using a mocking framework for .

Usage example: 

var handler = new TestHttpMessageHandler(request => Task.FromResult(new HttpResponseMessage(HttpStatusCode.OK)));

files.Title: BridgingIT.DevKit.Domain.ActiveEntityContext<TEntity, TId>
files.Summary:
Represents a context for Active Entity operations, bundling the provider and behaviors resolved from the same DI scope. This ensures that the provider and behaviors share the same lifetime and dependencies (e.g., DbContext).

Lifetime Warning: This context is only valid within the DI scope it was created from. Do not cache or pass it across async boundaries outside of that scope, as it may lead to if the underlying scope (and its services) are disposed. Always use it immediately within the creating method (e.g., inside or WithTransactionAsync<TEntity, TId>).

If you need to extend the context with more dependencies, resolve them at creation time and add them as properties here, rather than resolving them later to avoid scope mismatches.

This entity is configured to work with the Serilog.Sinks.MSSqlServer sink, mapping to a table named '__Logging_LogEntries' with standard columns as defined by the sink. files.Title: BridgingIT.DevKit.Infrastructure.EntityFramework.SqlServerIdempotentMigrationsSqlGenerator
files.Summary:
A custom SQL Server that emits idempotent DDL statements.

This generator wraps CREATE and DROP statements for tables, indexes, sequences, and constraints in IF EXISTS / IF NOT EXISTS guards, preventing errors when schema objects already exist or have been removed.

It allows repeated or parallel migration executions to remain resilient.

files.Title: BridgingIT.DevKit.Infrastructure.EntityFramework.SqliteSequenceNumberGenerator<TContext>
files.Summary: SQLite implementation using sqlite_sequence table with thread-safe operations. Note: SQLite does not support native sequences like SQL Server/PostgreSQL. Instead, it uses sqlite_sequence for AUTOINCREMENT tracking. This implementation emulates sequence behavior using the sqlite_sequence table. Schema is not supported in SQLite, so schema parameters are ignored but kept for interface consistency.
